name: Auto Push to GitHub on Save
description: Whenever a source file is saved in Kiro, auto-commit and push to GitHub main. GitHub Actions then builds and deploys to Netlify (ana-pulse.netlify.app) automatically.

then.prompt:
A source file was just saved in the InsightPulse project. Execute the Kiro → GitHub → Netlify pipeline using execute_pwsh:

Step 1 — Stage all changes:
git -C "C:\Users\prade\OneDrive\Desktop\InsightPulse\sentiment-ai-platform" add -A

Step 2 — Check for staged changes:
git -C "C:\Users\prade\OneDrive\Desktop\InsightPulse\sentiment-ai-platform" diff --cached --quiet

Step 3 — If there ARE staged changes (non-zero exit code), commit and push:
git -C "C:\Users\prade\OneDrive\Desktop\InsightPulse\sentiment-ai-platform" commit -m "auto: sync from Kiro IDE"
git -C "C:\Users\prade\OneDrive\Desktop\InsightPulse\sentiment-ai-platform" push origin main

If push succeeded, inform the user: 'Pushed to GitHub. GitHub Actions is now building and deploying to https://ana-pulse.netlify.app (~90 seconds). Monitor: https://github.com/P-V2705/INSIGHTPULSE/actions'

If no staged changes, do nothing silently.
